rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same scope.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the types of their assigned expressions.
Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for such assignments.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope and properly typed.
Use only declared constants, variables, or features in AGREE expressions to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Place implementation-level assignments in the correct annex scope to avoid scoping errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scopes should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Type declarations in AGREE must use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid scalar names.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined or unqualified identifiers in AGREE contracts; always ensure visibility in the current scope.
Do not mix incompatible types in AGREE expressions; ensure type consistency in declarations and assignments.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
